topic.what: Comprehensive review of the full clinico-pathological spectrum of gastritis, covering etiological diversity (H. pylori, autoimmune, chemical/reactive, lymphocytic, eosinophilic) and advocating for OLGA staging as a unifying risk stratification tool.
topic.how: Expert narrative review synthesizing histopathological classification systems, etiological categories, and clinical correlations, with emphasis on the OLGA staging system for atrophy assessment.
topic.why_matters: Gastritis encompasses a heterogeneous group of conditions with distinct etiologies and cancer risks; a unified clinico-pathological framework is essential for appropriate management and surveillance decisions.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Breadth of gastritis subtypes and etiologies covered in the review
characteristics.value: covers full etiological and histopathological spectrum
characteristics.vs_baseline: More comprehensive than single-etiology reviews; integrates OLGA staging across all gastritis subtypes
characteristics.evidence: Expert review synthesizing established classification systems (Sydney, OLGA, Houston-updated Sydney)
characteristics.context: Primarily relevant to histopathological assessment; requires biopsy-based diagnosis

good_for: Understanding the full differential diagnosis of gastritis
Applying OLGA staging consistently across different gastritis etiologies
Teaching and clinical reference for gastritis classification
not_for: Detailed molecular characterization of specific gastritis subtypes
Treatment protocol selection (focus is on classification, not therapy)
requires: Histopathological biopsy specimens with adequate sampling
Pathologist familiar with OLGA staging and gastritis classification systems

novelty: Advocates for OLGA staging as a universal risk stratification tool applicable across the full gastritis spectrum
Provides an integrated view of etiological diversity rather than treating gastritis subtypes in isolation
open_problems: Standardization of OLGA staging reproducibility across pathologists and institutions
Integration of molecular subtypes into the existing histopathological classification
Clinical validation of OLGA-based risk strata for non-H. pylori gastritis etiologies
